title: A 400th Episode "Ask Us Anything" Mailbag Extravaganza
format: The 400th-episode mailbag. Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins, with producer Bobby Wagner, take listener questions on Oscar bets, remakes best left alone, rewatchables wish lists, and the ranking of Nancy Meyers kitchens.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: The trailer dropped that morning; Sean watched it nine times with his daughter. Bradley Cooper as producer John Peters, plus Cooper Hoffman and Alana Haim in the leads.

films.title: Shakespeare in Love
films.note: Cited as the precedent for a supporting Oscar off little screen time: Judi Dench won for roughly eight minutes.

films.title: Network
films.note: Beatrice Straight's two scenes and one speech, the other short-role benchmark.

films.title: Boogie Nights
films.note: Licorice Pizza reads like a prelude to it: same San Fernando Valley, five years earlier.

films.title: Call Me by Your Name
films.note: Chalamet's nomination, in the who-hasn't-won-yet debate alongside Saoirse Ronan.

films.title: Legally Blonde
films.note: Amanda's 180: she dismissed it at 17 as a Harvard-bound nerd, now says it holds up completely.

films.title: Slacker
films.note: Sean's 180: he rented it from Blockbuster, thought it had no story, later understood Linklater's independent-film logic.

films.title: Dazed and Confused
films.note: The Linklater he'd already seen and loved before Slacker clicked.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: The retire-it-from-remakes anxiety case: Spielberg and Kushner, but without Jerome Robbins's choreography.

films.title: Casablanca
films.note: Their pick for a film that should never be remade; Bobby hasn't seen it, promises to for episode 500.

films.title: The Goldfinch
films.note: Evidence of how hard the beloved-novel adaptation is: the Donna Tartt failure.

films.title: Jaws
films.note: Good book, great movie: the mediocre-novel-to-great-film pattern.

films.title: The Stepford Wives
films.note: Another good-book, cool-movie example from the '70s.

films.title: The Notebook
films.note: Plot-first source material that still works on screen.

films.title: Hidden Figures
films.note: Theodore Melfi, raising nerves about his announced DeLillo adaptation.

films.title: The Starling
films.note: Melfi's just-released one, judged 'okay.'

films.title: White Noise
films.note: The DeLillo book Sean keeps wishing onto the screen: a then-upcoming Baumbach adaptation.

films.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
films.note: Bobby's rewatchables suggestion; cuts too hard for him to want on the mic. Sean argues they've never done right by Jim Carrey.

films.title: Midnight Run
films.note: Bill's long-standing rewatchables obsession.

films.title: Thief
films.note: The Michael Mann title the three of them converge on.

films.title: Forgetting Sarah Marshall
films.note: Craig Horlbeck's pick; Bobby's stated generation.

films.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
films.note: Amanda was promised it and it got taken away: nobody else at The Ringer wants to do it.

films.title: Sleepless in Seattle
films.note: A rom-com they've somehow never covered.

films.title: You've Got Mail
films.note: Amanda's fall rewatch, for the freshly-sharpened-pencils line.

films.title: Apollo 13
films.note: Amanda's most-rewatched film and her rewatchables wish.

films.title: Pulp Fiction
films.note: Among the classics held on ice for the feed.

films.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings
films.note: Only the 16th-biggest MCU movie but a real theatrical success, unreleased in China at the time.

films.title: Ant-Man
films.note: The MCU floor Shang-Chi cleared.

films.title: Halloween Kills
films.note: Universal's straight-to-Peacock gamble, the how-do-you-measure-success question.

films.title: Dear Evan Hansen
films.note: The theatrical flop the discourse tried to reframe as a streaming success.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Villeneuve as the other big-screen absolutist; a Denis interview teased for October.

films.title: Free Guy
films.note: Sean hated it; the scene he flagged had a moment on Twitter that weekend.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: In the 'piled-on but not actually garbage' bucket.

films.title: Malcolm & Marie
films.note: Same: overly maligned, not garbage.

films.title: Mank
films.note: One of the few films from the pandemic stretch they actually liked; Sean and Amanda are among its defenders.

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: The early-pandemic sense-memory of a barren release calendar.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: The 2019 run they miss: a big movie for both.

films.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
The Power of the Dog
films.note: The best of what Sean had seen so far that year.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: Just seen; opinion embargoed but not negative.

films.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
films.note: The 'a Venom movie's coming Friday' counterweight to the prestige slate.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Bobby's last big theatrical feeling; made $47M with Daniel Day-Lewis.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Bobby's dad-gearhead pick; Sean's shrug.

films.title: A Christmas Story
films.note: The example the question was built on, though not a Sean tradition.

films.title: The Muppet Christmas Carol
films.note: Sean's wife's mandatory Christmas Day watch.

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.note: In Sean's once-a-year personal rotation.

films.title: The Sound of Music
films.note: Amanda's yearly watch, on ABC with the commercials.

films.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
films.note: The joke Thanksgiving tradition for the kid.

films.title: Faces of Death
films.note: The joke Halloween pick for the kid.

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.note: The one film Sean insists his daughter must see; the mind-expander.

films.title: Bambi
films.note: The parental-death question: how early to expose a kid to the difficult stuff.

films.title: Star Wars
films.note: The childhood-imprint film for Amanda's husband.

films.title: Mary Poppins
films.note: Among the movies presented to Amanda as a child.

films.title: Goodfellas
films.note: What Bobby's parents sat him down for at 16, telling him 'here's your culture.'

films.title: The Maltese Falcon
films.note: A film worth being credited with, in the time-machine authorship game.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The 'take the money' answer to the same game.

films.title: Ocean's Eleven
films.note: Sean's pick: universally liked, and it makes you Soderbergh.

films.title: The Godfather
films.note: The framing example: you become Coppola at its release.

films.title: The Thomas Crown Affair
films.note: Bobby wants to be Pierce Brosnan's lifestyle in it.

films.title: American Gigolo
films.note: Sean's revised answer: one of Schrader's lonely men.

films.title: First Reformed
films.note: Another Schrader lonely-man option.

films.title: Bringing Up Baby
films.note: Amanda's Katharine Hepburn fantasy; the screwball register they don't make anymore.

films.title: Man on Fire
films.note: Bobby's half-joke answer for the film that built a recognizable world.

films.title: Adventureland
films.note: Bobby's real answer: Greg Mottola is from Long Island like him, and the setting scratched his brain.

films.title: Almost Famous
films.note: The one that made Bobby want to be a rock journalist.

films.title: Something's Gotta Give
films.note: Amanda's No. 1 Nancy Meyers kitchen: the pre-renovation Santa Barbara version.

films.title: It's Complicated
films.note: The archetypal Hamptons kitchen; Amanda insists it should never be expanded.

films.title: Father of the Bride
films.note: The lived-in '90s kitchen.

films.title: Baby Boom
films.note: Meyers on writing only, but the Vermont cabin kitchen counts.

films.title: The Holiday
films.note: Real-estate-forward; the Cotswold cottage kitchen isn't functional.

films.title: The Intern
films.note: Amanda's lowest-ranked kitchen: too HGTV-makeover.

films.title: Saw
films.note: Bobby's answer for 20 years of rooting for the Mets.

films.title: The Road
films.note: Sean goes bleaker: Hillcoat's McCarthy adaptation as the Mets metaphor.

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: Bobby's Criterion wish; the rewatchables entry he and Amanda missed.

films.title: Visions of Eight
films.note: The Criterion title they actually recorded a commentary for.

films.title: Inherent Vice
films.note: Amanda's PTA-minded Criterion wish: lots to unpack even if you don't love it.

films.title: Margaret
films.note: The first date Amanda still talks about: the three-hour Lonergan tragedy; they married anyway.

films.title: Grindhouse
films.note: Sean's most-fun four hours at the movies, alone.

films.title: Death Proof
films.note: Tarantino's half of the Grindhouse double feature.

films.title: Planet Terror
films.note: Rodriguez's half.

films.title: Robin Hood: Men in Tights
films.note: The one that sticks out: Sean at 11, his dad taking him and five friends, first sight of Dave Chappelle.

films.title: The Producers
films.note: The Mel Brooks portal that opened after Robin Hood.

films.title: The Many Saints of Newark
films.note: Teased at the close: the Sopranos prequel film driving the next episode.

films.title: What's Your Number?
films.note: The terrible Anna Faris film outside whose screening Amanda met her husband.

films.title: Space Camp
films.note: A recurring Amanda reference, invoked again in the rewatchables talk.

Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Ring.title: Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

Portrait of a Lady on Fire.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
